trending.title: EU must go beyond ‘binary yes or no ban’ on social media, top official says
trending.body: Decisionmakers tell POLITICO that social media ban for kids is ‘not the simple route.’

trending.title: Why Estonia is gambling on AI in schools
trending.body: Education and Research Minister Kristina Kallas is leading a pioneering effort to train students to use artificial intelligence.

trending.title: Von der Leyen, Hillary Clinton back new push to childproof AI
trending.body: New Youth AI Safety Institute plans to rate AI products based on how kids-friendly they are.

trending.title: China is going dark to develop its own Mythos, German cyber chief fears
trending.body: Several Chinese providers have recently stopped updating previously open AI models, which could indicate companies are moving development behind closed doors, Claudia Plattner warned.

trending.title: White House distances itself from tighter AI regulation
trending.body: The White House is looking for “partnership” with companies rather than pursuing “government regulation,” a senior White House official said.

privacy.title: EU must go beyond ‘binary yes or no ban’ on social media, top official says
privacy.body: Decisionmakers tell POLITICO that social media ban for kids is ‘not the simple route.’

regulatory.title: Draghi: Europe is ‘truly alone together’ in the age of Trump
regulatory.body: The continent needs to change rapidly in a world where the U.S. has become “more adversarial and unpredictable” and “may no longer guarantee our security,” says European grandee.

regulatory.title: OpenAI offers EU access to new AI hacking model
regulatory.body: European cyber and AI authorities in past weeks failed to gain access to superhacking AI, causing anxiety among officials.

regulatory.title: The far right’s crusade against French cinema
regulatory.body: French filmmakers fear a National Rally victory in next year’s presidential election will bring seismic changes to their industry.

regulatory.title: White House’s ‘lack of organization’ has AI lobbyists fretting
regulatory.body: The Trump administration has sent mixed signals about how tough it intends to be in vetting powerful new computer models.

regulatory.title: Sovereignty push shouldn’t disrupt tech supply chains, EU innovation chief warns
regulatory.body: Some in the U.S. still see value in working with European tech talent, Ekaterina Zaharieva says.

regulatory.title: Lawmakers, industry push EU to move faster on AI rule cuts
regulatory.body: Thursday’s deal leaves some lawmakers and lobby groups dissatisfied.

regulatory.title: EU agreement on US trade deal within reach, says top lawmaker
regulatory.body: “I guess there will be a conclusion at the next meeting,” says German MEP Bernd Lange.

regulatory.title: EU clinches deal to roll back AI restrictions
regulatory.body: Deal marks first significant delay of digital rules amid pressure from the U.S.

regulatory.title: Belgium urges EU to save industry by getting tough on China
regulatory.body: On returning from China, Foreign Minister Maxime Prévot warns the EU will become a “coalition of frogs” unless it embraces a cohesive strategy.

regulatory.title: Germany set to secure win for industry in AI talks
regulatory.body: Exemption for industry would mark a dramatic departure from law’s initial setup.

regulatory.title: US firms could face exclusion under new EU cyber bill, lead lawmaker says
regulatory.body: “I can see the Cybersecurity Act having an impact on U.S. companies if they don’t oblige by the rules,” says Czech Pirate MEP Markéta Gregorová.

regulatory.title: Cloud law will stop Europe becoming tech ‘colony,’ Commission official says
regulatory.body: The fresh comments suggest Brussels plans to take a tough approach to wean the continent off foreign technology.

regulatory.title: European industry fears ‘back door’ for US in cloud law
regulatory.body: A technical formula on what counts as sovereign is prompting fierce reaction.

regulatory.title: EU pressure builds on Anthropic over Mythos hacking risks
regulatory.body: Officials are losing patience over lack of access to superhacking model.

regulatory.title: Key EU clash looms over Trump trade deal
regulatory.body: The U.S. president’s threat to hike tariffs on European autos supports the judgment of a majority of EU lawmakers that he can’t be trusted.

regulatory.title: PornHub lets UK Apple users back on its platform
regulatory.body: PornHub partially blocked access for U.K. users in January over concerns about the Online Safety Act.

regulatory.title: Europe’s laws ‘ill-equipped’ to deal with superhacking AI, lawmakers warn
regulatory.body: EU cyber agency ENISA needs access to Mythos to scrutinize the risks, lawmakers said.

regulatory.title: UK seeks access to EU’s €5B tech scale-up fund

regulatory.title: A runaway tycoon and millions in crypto: Polish scandal stokes Tusk-Nawrocki fight

regulatory.title: EU accused of wasting €20B on AI computing dreams
regulatory.body: Critics argue that there’s no demand for the massive computing hubs Brussels has planned.
